subject: Public Health
title: Famine: Causes Beyond Drought
description: A fast breakdown of Famine: Causes Beyond Drought for builders.

hook.text: Most famines aren't caused by nature. They're caused by war, politics, and failed distribution.

buildup.text: Amartya Sen showed that famines occur even when food exists—it's access, not supply, that fails.

discovery.text: The Bengal Famine of 1943 killed 3 million—India was exporting rice while its people starved.

twist.text: Today, the world produces enough food for 10 billion people—yet 735 million go hungry.

climax.text: Famine is a political failure, not a technical one. Every modern famine has human decision-making at its core.

punchline.text: The world has enough food. It lacks the will to share.

quiz.question: According to Amartya Sen, what primarily causes famines?
options: Failures of access and distribution, not food supply
